goal: Implement P1.1: make the orchestrate loop's pause button actually halt execution. The pause_requested flag exists on OrchestrateRun and the UI endpoint flips it, but the executor loop never checks it — users click Pause yet the run keeps consuming tasks and budget.

tasks.name: Scaffold minimal platform workspace
tasks.description: Create platform/ directory with stubs needed for tests.
tasks.instruction: Create platform/app/api/pipeline.py with orchestrate() function and while-loop stub, plus _check_pause stub. Create platform/app/api/tier1.py stub. Create platform/app/models/orchestrate_run.py with OrchestrateRun dataclass (id, status, pause_requested, paused_at, resumed_at, tasks_completed, tasks_failed, total_cost_usd, current_task_external_id, current_phase, progress_message, error, result, params, cancel_requested). Create platform/app/templates/_orchestrate_panel.html stub. Copy tests/test_p1_pause.py from platform source. Add requirements.txt and conftest.py.

acceptance_criteria.text: pytest --collect-only succeeds without import errors on tests/test_p1_pause.py

tasks.name: Add _check_pause() helper and cooperative gate in orchestrate loop
tasks.description: Cooperative pause gate so executor halts between tasks when pause_requested=True.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/api/pipeline.py: add _check_pause(db, run_id) that calls db.expire_all() then reads pause_requested fresh. Insert gate at top of orchestrate while-loop (before claiming next task). On pause: set status=PAUSED, paused_at=now(), current_phase=paused, break. finished_at stays NULL. Return {tasks_run, results, stopped_reason: paused}.

acceptance_criteria.text: _check_pause(db, run_id) returns True when pause_requested=True (fresh read)
Orchestrate loop sets status=PAUSED when flag is set

produces.helper: _check_pause(db, run_id) -> bool — cooperative gate for orchestrate loop

tasks.name: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/pause endpoint
tasks.description: Sets pause_requested=True, rejects 409 for terminal states.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/api/tier1.py: add POST /orchestrate-runs/{run_id}/pause. Sets pause_requested=True. Rejects 409 for terminal states (DONE/FAILED/CANCELLED/BUDGET_EXCEEDED/PARTIAL_FAIL). Returns {run_id, pause_requested: true, status}.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /pause returns 200 with pause_requested=true for RUNNING run

tasks.name: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume endpoint
tasks.description: PAUSED runs get respawned; RUNNING+flag just clears the flag.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/api/tier1.py: add POST /orchestrate-runs/{run_id}/resume. PAUSED: clear flag, set resumed_at=now(), set status=RUNNING, respawn background worker. RUNNING+pause_requested: clear flag only, no respawn. Terminal: 409. Returns {run_id, pause_requested: false, respawned: bool}.

tasks.name: Expose pause fields in run status endpoint and render Resume in UI panel
tasks.description: GET /orchestrate-runs/{id} returns pause fields; UI panel shows Resume button for PAUSED runs.
tasks.instruction: Ensure platform/app/api/pipeline.py GET /orchestrate-runs/{id} returns pause_requested, paused_at, resumed_at. Ensure platform/app/templates/_orchestrate_panel.html renders PAUSED status with Resume button linking to /api/v1/tier1/orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume. Integration tests confirm both.

acceptance_criteria.text: UI panel shows PAUSED and Resume button with correct resume URL
